expectations.evidence: All required sections appear: Problem, Solution, Scope, Technical Approach, Risks, Alternatives, and Verification.
No file-line references matching patterns like 'file.ts:123' were found.
No fenced ts/tsx/typescript/javascript/js code blocks are present.
Spec is 101 lines, under the 120-line maximum.

Core implementation still requires clarification on a foundational dependency (where user timezone is persisted/defaulted), and digest scope semantics are unresolved, so a full core flow cannot be implemented without follow-up questions.
Not every in-scope behavior has explicit trigger conditions in Technical Approach (e.g., due-soon threshold behavior and digest-click-to-filter interaction are only partially specified there).
Technical Approach paragraphs lead with clear decisions (derived UI due-state, durable backend overdue state, canonical timezone source).
Does not repeatedly use formulaic decision-announcing phrases; only isolated usage appears, below failure threshold.

Includes implementation-level mechanics and algorithmic detail (code-path/file references, UTC window batching, per-timezone cohorts, staleness guard behavior) instead of staying purely at behavior/policy level.
Two failure modes are present and both specify policy decisions (read-path staleness correction fallback; split global overdue vs user-relative due-today semantics).
Out-of-scope section names realistic adjacent creep risks: outbound channels, custom reminder schedules/snooze, escalation automation, and analytics/reporting.
